creator: fenner@parc.xerox.com
text:
In message <199903032003.PAA13426@tuzik.lz.att.com> you write:
>	I have set ftp_proxy and http_proxy in the environment (using
>	IP numbers).

Sigh.  fetch had FTP_PROXY and HTTP_PROXY first; then the NetBSD ftp
got imported which uses ftp_proxy and http_proxy, and now we have two
different ways to do the same thing.

>	What was the reason to use `fetch' rather than `ftp' as
>	FETCH_CMD in /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.port.mk?

"ftp" did not support http: URL's (since it's ... uh ... "ftp").

  Bill
